process_issues.description: Verification-surface gap (recurring, 2x this run): the build-target e2e runs the compiled web bundle in Playwright/Chromium, which does NOT exercise the Electron runtime. Two real bugs shipped past verification-gate PASS and were caught only in manual Ableton smoke: (1) 1-beat clip length, (2) window.prompt unsupported in Electron. Addressed by verification-gate Required Evidence #9 (packaged-runtime parity) added this retro.

observed_chain: coding-agent x4 (server / client / install / create-track — all returned validation reports, no premature project-state rewrite — the 2026-06-05 coding-agent SKILL fix held)
manual-smoke (2 FAIL → repair → re-verify, then PASS)

issues.description: verification-gate emitted PASS twice on changes whose user-visible behavior only manifests in the Electron runtime (clip length needs a live Ableton; window.prompt is disabled in Electron). The build-target e2e (Chromium) cannot catch these. The packaged-target smoke (test:e2e:build) was treated as optional/post-hoc rather than a required gate for renderer/Electron changes.
